items.title: Perikatan to contest all 56 Johor seats, open to Muda collaboration in upcoming state polls
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 4 — The Opposition Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition intends to contest all 56 state assembly seats in the upcoming Johor state election (PRN). Johor Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) chief Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal said PN and its parties are prepared to face the state election at any time. “PN will contest all the state constituency seats in Johor.

items.title: 1,453 migrants detained, Interpol fugitive and 'flying passport' ring exposed in Johor blitz
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A South Korean man wanted under an Interpol Red Notice and a "flying passport" syndicate involving Vietnamese travel documents are among six major cases uncovered in Johor's immigration enforcement blitz. State Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said that on April 8, enforcement officers raided a luxury residence in Horizon Hills, Iskandar Puteri, and detained a South Korean man listed under an Interpol Red Notice for alleged fraud involving a face mask business scandal in his home country. Rusdi said the man's passport had been revoked by the Republic of Korea, and investigations showed he had overstayed in Malaysia following the cancellation of his travel document.

items.title: Only 0.1pct of Kedah padi fields planted amid heatwave
items.summary: ALOR STAR: Only 0.1 per cent of padi fields under the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) have begun planting due to the prolonged heatwave and low rainfall. Mada chairman Datuk Dr Ismail Salleh said that although the planting rate is low, more than half of the areas are ready for cultivation. Ismail said the situation directly affects the ability to release water to support padi planting activities.

items.title: Ministry explores tube well development to safeguard against future water shortages
items.summary: LIPIS: The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry is conducting a study on the development of tube wells as a preparatory measure to address potential water crisis in the future. Deputy Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the study, carried out by the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia (NAHRIM), aims to identify new water sources as a support plan in the event of supply disruptions. "This high level of water usage must be reduced.

items.title: Zahid: Abu Dhabi's Lulu Group to buy RM400mil of Malaysian halal products
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Abu Dhabi-based retail giant Lulu Group plans to procure halal products worth US$100 million (over RM400 million) from Malaysia, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said the initiative would open a significant pathway for Malaysian halal products – particularly those produced by Bumiputera entrepreneurs – to penetrate international markets. He said that he had proposed, in Anwar's presence, for Lulu Group to work directly with Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) in sourcing halal products for global distribution.

items.title: Iran foreign ministry says US must 'abandon its excessive demands'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said today that the United States must reduce its demands on the Islamic republic, with negotiations stalled over ending the two-month-long Middle East war. "At this stage, our priority is to end the war," he said in a briefing broadcast by state television. "The other side must commit to a reasonable approach and abandon its excessive demands regarding Iran."

items.title: Rosmaini to face fresh charges after getting maximum 14-year sentence
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Trader Rosmaini Abdul Raof, whose prison sentence was increased to the maximum of 14 years by the Court of Appeal for an assault that left his pregnant ex-wife in a coma, is set to face new charges. Lawyer Kamal Hisham Ja'afar said Rosmaini, 43, is expected to face the charges at the Alor Star courts. Kamal Hisham said Rosmaini accepted the appellate court's ruling with an open heart.

items.title: Zahid sets RM1bil halal export target for Mara next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has instructed Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) to push sales of its halal products in the international market beyond RM1 billion next year. Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said the target was necessary to move Bumiputera halal entrepreneurs beyond the domestic market and into the global halal supply chain. He said Mara-linked halal products had recorded RM819 million in potential sales through international halal forum engagements, along with 13 memorandums of understanding worth more than RM70 million.

items.title: Factbox: What is the hantavirus that killed three cruise ship passengers?
items.summary: PRAIA, Cape Verde: Three people have died and three are ill after a Netherlands-based cruise ship was hit by a suspected outbreak of hantavirus , authorities and media reports said on Sunday. Spread by rodents Hantavirus is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people in rare cases, according to the World Health Organization. According to the Canadian government, about 200 cases of hantavirus pulmonary illness occur each year worldwide.

items.title: Sumud Nusantara Command Centre begins seven-day countdown for second Gaza flotilla
items.summary: SEPANG: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has officially initiated a seven-day countdown for the second wave of its "Break the Silence and Break the Siege" campaign, signalling that a fresh humanitarian flotilla mission to Gaza is imminent. SNCC director Datuk Dr Sani Araby Alim Araby said the countdown serves as a "call to arms" for readiness rather than a fixed departure date. It is not strictly fixed at seven days," he told reporters at a SNCC press conference today.

items.title: Health Ministry's development budget safe despite austerity drive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry's development budget will not be affected by the government's austerity drive, with funding for essential healthcare infrastructure and services set to be preserved. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said while the ministry remained open to trimming non-essential expenditure, development allocations and core services would continue to be prioritised. He also expressed appreciation to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for agreeing to review a proposed RM3.04 billion budget cut to the ministry.

items.title: Tiffin carrier becomes symbol of appreciation for Pahang's 'red heroes'
items.summary: KUANTAN: A familiar household item, the mangkuk tingkat, is taking on a special role this World Firefighters' Day, as the tradition of sending meals in tiered tiffin carriers is repurposed to express appreciation and support for firefighters on duty. The initiative by the Fire and Rescue Department's Women Officers and Firefighters' Wives Association (Perisma) Pahang features a special edition tiffin carrier in recognition of firefighters' round-the-clock commitment to public safety. The tiffin carrier, also known as siya, incorporates design elements representing women firefighters, highlighting their role within the rescue force.

items.title: Pahang MB to meet Taiping Zoo over fate of Malaysian elephants at Japan’s Tennoji Zoo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said he will hold a meeting with the Taiping Zoo and Night Safari in the near future to discuss issues related to three elephants, Dara, Amoi and Kelat (DAK), currently at Tennoji Zoo, Japan. “I, along with the Taiping Zoo authorities, will hold a discussion and meeting in the near future, even earlier than the meeting planned by the Perak state government. — Bernama

items.title: Perak govt extends counselling support to family of teen stabbed 61 times
items.summary: PARIT BUNTAR: The state government has mobilised counselling support for the family of a teenage girl who was killed in a brutal stabbing incident, with 61 wounds found on her body. State Women, Family, Social Welfare and Entrepreneur Development Committee chairman Datuk Salbiah Mohamed said the Social Welfare Department had deployed psychologists to provide trauma counselling to the victim's family in Kuala Kangsar. Salbiah said department officers had visited the family earlier to offer immediate psychological support and would continue to monitor their well-being.

items.title: King extends International Firefighters' Day greetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended International Firefighters' Day 2026 greetings to personnel of the Fire and Rescue Department. In a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's Facebook page, His Majesty expressed appreciation for the service, sacrifices, and steadfast commitment of the Red Heroes, who are always ready to risk their lives for public safety and national well-being. Sultan Ibrahim also expressed pride in the vital role the department plays in handling emergencies, from fires and natural disasters to rescue operations.

items.title: Backbenchers Club urge mitigation plan over proposed RM3b health budget restructuring
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The Madani Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) has urged the government to present a comprehensive mitigation plan following public concern over proposed projections for the restructuring of the national budget, particularly involving the health sector, which is estimated to reach RM3 billion. She said that to ensure the sustainability of the national healthcare system in the medium and long term, the implementation of initiatives outlined in the Health White Paper must be expedited, including a shift towards a more comprehensive, wellbeing-based healthcare approach. She said efforts to strengthen local production capacity for medicines and medical devices must also be enhanced to ensure the country’s health supply security.

items.title: Petai smuggling busted in Kelantan border raid
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: The General Operations Force (GOF) detained a man and seized 30 packets of smuggled petai during an operation at Kampung Lubok Gong here yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was made by the Ninth Battalion's Strike Force during routine border patrols at 5.30pm. "Checks revealed plastic packets containing petai without valid documentation.

items.title: Media Prima contributes RM10,000 to Pekan girls' home
items.summary: PEKAN: Media Prima Bhd (MPB) has contributed RM10,000 to the Darussalam Al Mukhtar Home for orphans and underprivileged girls here to support education for its beneficiaries. MPB group chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussian Aljunid handed over the mock cheque to the home's caretaker, Nik Ibrahim Nik Abdullah, in a ceremony witnessed by the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah. Also present were Tengku Mahkota Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah.

items.title: Death toll from Turkiye school shooting rises to 10
items.summary: ISTANBUL: An 11-year-old girl has died of injuries sustained in a school shooting in southern Turkiye more than two weeks ago, bringing the death toll to 10, media reported Monday. Almina Agaoglu died in hospital, the private NTV television reported. A 14-year-old opened fire at a school in Kahramanmaras province on April 15, killing nine students aged 10 and 11 and one teacher.

items.title: PNB's 118 Mall set for August debut
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A major new retail landmark is taking shape beneath the world's second-tallest tower, as 118 Mall prepares for its official opening this August in the heart of the Merdeka 118 precinct. Owned and managed by PNB Merdeka Ventures Sdn Bhd, a subsidiary of Permodalan Nasional Bhd, the mall is positioned within one of Kuala Lumpur's most high-profile urban developments. 118 Mall retail head Sue Wang said the tenant mix was carefully curated to reflect Kuala Lumpur's identity.

items.title: Woman's home vandalised, family threatened over unpaid debt by loan shark
items.summary: JASIN: A female trader faced a terrifying ordeal when her rented house in Taman Scientex Jasin Mutiara was allegedly harassed by loan sharks on Saturday. In the 2pm incident, the suspect allegedly threw a bottle of chilli sauce at the house and a brick at the windshield of the victim's Proton Persona. The suspect began threatening the woman after she allegedly failed to make repayments, including sending pictures of her family's house in Pontian, Johor, and threatening to reveal her personal information.

items.title: Fisherman finds 'whale vomit', says burnt sample gave off fragrant smoke
items.summary: KEMAMAN: A fisherman has come across what he believes could be a rare lump of whale vomit after discovering a wax-like object weighing about 20kg while squid jigging at sea. Mohd Raffi Yusoff, 58, said he found the floating object about four nautical miles off Kuala Kemasik around 6pm last Thursday. Raffi said the object continued to emit a fragrant scent throughout the night while he continued squid jigging.

items.title: Malaysia can withstand external shocks: Treasury deputy sec-gen
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia has the capacity to withstand external economic shocks amid a more uncertain global landscape, supported by sound fundamentals and ongoing reforms, the Finance Ministry said. Treasury deputy secretary-general (investment) Datuk Dr Shahrazat Ahmad said the global outlook is increasingly clouded by geopolitical tensions, supply chain shifts and rapid technological change, which are altering traditional patterns of trade and investment. Representing Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican, she said Malaysia's openness to global markets exposes it to these shifts, but also positions the country to tap emerging opportunities.

items.title: Sabah tourism resilient: New global hotel chains to create hundreds of jobs
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sabah government is ramping up efforts to bolster the state's tourism capacity, ensuring the industry remains resilient despite the ongoing Middle East crisis. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said key initiatives include establishing partnerships with global airlines to enhance connectivity, developing premium tourism products, and elevating service standards to international benchmarks. These developments are set to add 2,740 rooms to the state's inventory—a testament, Hajiji said, to the industry's optimism during uncertain times.

items.title: Johor ramps up water security measures as drought risk intensifies in Sedili, Johor Lama
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor government has stepped up long-term water security initiatives for Sedili and Johor Lama as prolonged dry weather and the rising risk of meteorological drought threaten raw water stability across the state. This poses a direct risk to the raw water supply," he told the Johor State Legislative Assembly sitting here today. He said immediate mitigation efforts are already in place, including water tanker deliveries, static water tanks, bottled water distribution and cloud seeding operations.

items.title: Kedah KPDN foils illegal diesel transfer at Chinese cemetery
items.summary: Kedah KPDN Enforcement Chief Zalina Aziz said during the 2pm raid, authorities discovered diesel being transferred from a cargo lorry into a tanker lorry. Two men in their 40s and 50s, who were transferring diesel from a cargo lorry fitted with an intermediate bulk container (IBC) tank at the rear into a tanker lorry, were arrested. “KPDN also seized approximately 2,900 litres of diesel worth RM44,848.

items.title: NGO head denies defaming Mais in 3R-linked court case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The president of a non-governmental organisation pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court to allegedly defaming the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (Mais). Mohd Ridzuan Abdullah, 58, is accused of making defamatory statements linked to purported arrears payments for mosque construction, allegedly to harm Mais' reputation, as well as misusing the name of the palace. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar, who appeared with Nor Azizah Aling, asked the court to set a RM10,000 with one surety bail.

items.title: Putrajaya dengue cases drop 36.2pc, Wolbachia method shows success
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — Dengue cases in Putrajaya fell by 36.2 per cent from January to April 2026 compared with the same period last year, with the implementation of Wolbachia technology identified as a key factor in enabling faster and more effective control of the urban outbreak. Putrajaya Corporation (PPj) president Datuk Mohd Sakeri Abdul Kadir said the decrease shows that conventional approaches alone are no longer sufficient to address dengue challenges in densely populated urban areas. “Data in Putrajaya shows that innovations such as the Wolbachia technology can accelerate dengue control and protect communities more effectively.

items.title: Deputy finance minister: Malaysia must become tech-driven nation to tackle low wages
items.summary: Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the issue can be tackled if companies in the country, including in Johor, have more technological innovations, thereby creating jobs that offer higher salaries to locals. “Both the state and federal governments need to share responsibility in developing Malaysian companies into multinational corporations,” he said during the Johor state legislative assembly sitting at the Sultan Ismail Building here today. Liew, who is also the Iskandar Puteri Member of Parliament, said many residents living in Greater Johor Bahru have expressed dissatisfaction about the rising cost of living following the influx of Singaporeans and corporate clients into Johor, which has become increasingly prosperous of late.

items.title: Court sets Dec 1 for Inspector Sheila obstruction of duty trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The magistrate's court here today set two days beginning Dec 1 for the trial of suspended police officer Inspector Sheila, who is charged with obstructing a policeman. Magistrate Aina Azahra Arifin fixed the dates after deputy public prosecutor Hench Goh informed the court that a representation submitted by Sheila, whose real name is Sheila Sharon Steven Kumar, 38, had been rejected by the Attorney-General's Chambers. At today's proceedings, Sheila was represented by lawyer M. Manoharan.

items.title: Island operators hit by soaring fuel costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Island operators across Malaysia are facing mounting operational pressures due to fuel costs rising by 50-70 per cent, placing severe strain on off-grid tourism businesses. Perhentian Island Tourism Operators Association (5P) said rising fuel prices, combined with continued reliance on off-grid power generation is hurting island based operators. 5P chairperson Rafizah Munir said operating costs on the islands have risen significantl in recent months, driven largely by escalating fuel prices.

items.title: Chegubard apologises to Nik Nazmi, admits his allegations were false, baseless
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A defamation suit filed by former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad against activist cum blogger Badrul Hisham Shaharin has been resolved after the latter issued a public apology at the High Court here today. Badrul Hisham, better known as Chegubard, read out the consent judgment, acknowledging that his allegations against Nik Nazmi were false and baseless. "I withdraw all allegations against Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad.

items.title: Knife-wielding robber flees with RM1,200 cash after threatening convenience store worker in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING, May 4 — Police are on the lookout for a man believed to have been involved in a knife-point robbery at a 24-hour convenience store in Kota Sentosa earlier today. Padawan district police chief Supt Mohd Irwan Hafiz Md Razdi said the incident took place at around 4.30am. “The suspect is a man armed with a knife who was on foot.

items.title: Freak storm batters Pontian, fells trees in JB as volatile weather grips Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A freak afternoon storm swept through Kampung Melayu Raya in Pekan Nenas, Pontian yesterday, ripping off roofs and forcing families to flee as violent winds and heavy rain struck with little warning. Several residents described moments of panic as the storm intensified within minutes. State Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Muhammad Tarmizi Md Jaafar said the department responded to one storm-related incident and three cases of fallen trees following the squall.

items.title: Sabah Customs seizes RM15m worth of methamphetamine at Sepanggar Port
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 4 — The Sabah Customs Department has thwarted an attempt to smuggle 305.722kg of methamphetamine worth over RM15 million into the state. Sabah Customs director Dr Ahmad Taufik Sulaman said acting on intelligence information, officers inspected a container which had arrived at Sepanggar Port from Port Klang on April 22. Ahmad Taufik also thanked the public for their continuous cooperation in combating smuggling involving cigarettes, liquor, firecrackers, vehicles, and drugs.

items.title: Knife-wielding woman at Rain Rave charged, ordered to undergo psychiatric assessment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — A 28-year-old woman arrested after allegedly brandishing a knife during the final night of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in Bukit Bintang has been charged in the Sessions Court today with carrying an offensive weapon in public. The charge was framed under Section 6(1) of the Corrosive and Explosive Substances and Offensive Weapons Act for carrying offensive weapons in a public place, which carries a jail term of between five and 10 years, as well as whipping, upon conviction. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said the woman, identified as Shannon Anne-Ooi, had been ordered by the court to undergo psychiatric assessment at Hospital Bahagia in Ulu Kinta, Perak, due to suspected mental health issues.

items.title: Tour bus carrying Indian tourists veers off PLUS expressway near Alor Gajah, injuring driver and two children
items.summary: He said the bus was carrying nine people — eight Indian tourists and a local male driver. The passengers comprised three adult men, including the driver, three adult women and three boys. “Three victims suffered minor injuries involving the driver and two boys.

items.title: Factory worker dies after collapsing on Gunung Lambak
items.summary: KLUANG: A factory worker died after losing consciousness at a height of 800 metres from the base of Gunung Lambak here yesterday. Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the victim, Muhamad Nur Hakimi Mohd Haris, 32, from Felda Sungai Sibol, Kulai was descending the hill with three of his friends when he lost consciousness. "Heavy rain complicated the operation and Muhamad Nur Hakimi was brought down at 6.15pm, and was pronounced dead by a medical assistant officer," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Tears roll down Jahidah's cheeks when informed of her ex-husband's jail term — Sister
items.summary: Jahidah Nordin, 48, reportedly reacted emotionally after her sister, Fauziah Nordin, 45, informed her of the court's decision involving her ex-husband, Rosmaini Abdul Raof. Fauziah said she was deeply emotional upon reading the ruling and immediately went to her sister to share the news. Fauziah said the additional sentence and compensation imposed on Rosmaini may be the result of Jahidah's prayers, as she was the most severely wronged.

items.title: 'Ibu… forgive me' — teen who attacked adoptive mother pleads for mercy
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An 18-year-old youth pleaded for forgiveness from his adoptive mother after being charged in the magistrate's court here today with assaulting her and damaging household property when she refused to give him money. I want to bow at your feet," he said before magistrate Arun Noval Dass after pleading guilty to two charges under Section 323 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 326A, and Section 427 of the same code. On the second charge, he was accused of committing mischief by damaging a door, sofa, wall mirror, dining chair and flower pots belonging to the victim, causing losses estimated at RM5,400.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Adverse weather conditions expected across much of the country until evening
items.summary: In an alert issued at 2.30pm, MetMalaysia said the affected areas include Perlis, as well as Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu) and Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan). The same warning also covers Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Mualim), Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin) and Selangor (Hulu Selangor and Hulu Langat). In Sarawak, the affected areas include Sri Aman, Betong (Betong), Sarikei (Pakan), Sibu (Sibu and Selangau), Mukah (Daro, Matu, Dalat and Mukah), Bintulu (Tatau and Bintulu), Miri (Subis, Miri and Marudi), as well as Limbang.

items.title: Chegubard apologises to Nik Nazmi in court, defamation suit settled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — A defamation suit filed by former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad against activist cum blogger Badrul Hisham Shaharin has been resolved after the latter issued a public apology at the High Court here today. Badrul Hisham, better known as Chegubard, read out the consent judgment, acknowledging that his allegations against Nik Nazmi were false and baseless. “I withdraw all allegations against Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad.

items.title: Cropmate says some frozen bank accounts released by MACC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Cropmate Bhd said it was notified by its solicitor on May 1, 2026, that some of the group and its subsidiary’s bank accounts have been partially released by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). It said the bank accounts were previously frozen under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001. Last Tuesday (April 28), Cropmate said it had confirmed that RM12.21 million in bank accounts belonging to the company and its subsidiary Cropmate Fertilizer Sdn Bhd had been frozen by the MACC as of April 24, 2026, and that it was actively taking the necessary steps to address the matter.

items.title: Anthony Loke’s pol-sec lodges police report against Chegubard over alleged defamatory remarks
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 4 — The political secretary to Transport Minister Anthony Loke today lodged a police report against Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, over a social media post allegedly containing defamatory remarks against Loke involving the Undang institution in Negeri Sembilan. The report was lodged by Ho Weng Wah at the Seremban District Police Headquarters at noon following a Facebook post by Chegubard titled “Anthony Loke must be investigated for funding an illegal entity – Majlis Dato Lembaga Adat Sungei Ujong.” “The post contains allegations that Transport Minister Loke Siew Fook had channelled a substantial amount of funds to an entity claimed to be illegal and links the matter to disturbances involving the royal institution in this state. Ho said firm action must be taken to curb the spread of false information and defamatory content, particularly those involving sensitive 3R issues, in order to safeguard national harmony and stability.

items.title: Zahid pays tribute to Bomba personnel for courage and sacrifice on International Firefighters’ Day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has expressed appreciation to personnel of the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) for their unwavering commitment, discipline and sacrifices. In his message in conjunction with International Firefighters’ Day today, he said every moment of emergency demands extraordinary courage, and it is in such critical times that firefighters step forward without hesitation to shoulder the responsibility of protecting lives and property. “In conjunction with International Firefighters’ Day celebrated today, I would like to record my appreciation to all JBPM personnel for their commitment, discipline and sacrifices,” he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: PETRA explores tube well development to safeguard against future water shortages
items.summary: LIPIS, May 4 — The Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA) is conducting a study on the development of tube wells as a preparatory measure to address potential water crisis in the future. PETRA deputy minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the study, carried out by the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia (NAHRIM), aims to identify new water sources as a support plan in the event of supply disruptions. “This high level of water usage must be reduced.

items.title: Trailer driver remanded after fatal LPT2 crash near Chukai toll plaza
items.summary: Kemaman police chief Supt Mohd Razi Rosli said the remand order against the 47-year-old man from Permaisuri, Setiu, was issued by Magistrate Marsha Abdul Malik at the Kemaman Magistrate’s Court here this morning. “The suspect, who sustained minor injuries, was discharged from Kemaman Hospital yesterday and was brought to the Magistrate’s Court this morning to obtain the remand order. Mohd Razi said the man is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Factory worker dies after losing consciousness while descending Gunung Lambak
items.summary: KLUANG, May 4 — A factory worker died after losing consciousness at a height of 800 metres from the base of Gunung Lambak here yesterday. Kluang district police chief ACP Bahrin Mohd Noh said the victim, Muhamad Nur Hakimi Mohd Haris, 32, from Felda Sungai Sibol, Kulai was descending the hill with three of his friends when he lost consciousness. "Heavy rain complicated the operation and Muhamad Nur Hakimi was brought down at about 6.15pm, and was pronounced dead by a medical assistant officer,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Court of Appeal rules emergency proclamations beyond judicial review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya today ruled that emergency proclamations issued by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong cannot be challenged in court, reaffirming that the Federal Constitution bars judicial scrutiny over the monarch’s decision on whether a grave emergency exists. According to the New Straits Times , Federal Court judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah said Article 150(8) of the Federal Constitution expressly states that the King’s decision is “final and conclusive” and cannot be questioned by the courts. “The court cannot exercise its judicial power on national security,” he said when delivering the Court of Appeal’s unanimous decision today.

items.title: Kelantan police turn complainants over fake PDRM posts linked to Ketereh murder case
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 4 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has detected the use of its official logo without authorisation by irresponsible parties to spread unverified information regarding the discovery of the body of a 19-year-old woman with multiple stab wounds near a paddy field in Kampung Simah, Ketereh. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the tactic was deliberately carried out to create the impression that the content was an official statement from the PDRM. Following the circulation of various unverified information on social media, a police report has been lodged.

items.title: December trial date set for ‘Inspector Sheila’ after AGC rejects representation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The Magistrates’ Court here today fixed two days beginning Dec 1 for the trial of a police officer known as “Inspector Sheila”, who is accused of obstructing police officers from carrying out their duties outside a restaurant in November last year. The woman is accused of intentionally obstructing a police officer, Lance Corporal Malvinderjit Singh Teerath Singh, 37, from carrying out his crime prevention patrol duties outside a restaurant on Jalan Sultan Azlan Shah, Dang Wangi, between 8.15pm and 8.30pm on November 4 last year. Inspector Sheila has been suspended from duty since June 23, 2023, following several cases, including allegedly insulting a police officer and threatening an elderly woman.

items.title: Viral Simpang Pulai R&R parking clash sparks outrage after bus driver allegedly spat on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — A viral video showing a confrontation between a bus driver and several motorists at the Simpang Pulai rest-and-service area (R&R) has triggered widespread backlash online, with many Malaysians condemning the alleged treatment of the driver. The 42-second clip, which has been widely circulated on social media, shows several vehicles parked in what appeared to be a designated bus parking area, allegedly obstructing access for buses. Many commenters expressed sympathy for the driver, arguing that bus parking bays should be respected to avoid disrupting public transport operations.

items.title: Media veterans visit ailing laureate Adibah Amin
items.summary: SUBANG, May 4 — Three prominent Malaysian media figures paid a heartfelt visit to fellow National Journalism Laureate, Adibah Amin, who is currently unwell. Tan Sri Johan Jaafar, Datuk Abdul Kadir Jasin, and Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai called on the 90-year-old veteran journalist at her home to convey their well-wishes and support. Past recipients include Tan Sri A. Samad Ismail, Tan Sri Lee Siew Yee, Tan Sri Melan Abdullah, Tan Sri Mazlan Nordin, Tan Sri Zainuddin Maidin and Datuk Ishak Muhammad.

items.title: Sabah tourism can weather Middle East crisis, says Hajiji as new hotel investments roll in
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 4 — Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Haji Noor is confident the state’s tourism sector will remain resilient despite challenges posed by the current Middle East conflict. He said that while the government has been refining its strategies, continued investor interest in the state showed Sabah would be able to weather the challenges. Hajiji said the state tourism ministry had stepped up efforts to ensure Sabah remains an attractive global destination in response to emerging industry challenges.

items.title: Penang manufacturer fined RM120,000 after workers suffer hearing loss from industrial noise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — A manufacturing company was fined RM120,000 by the Sessions Court in Butterworth today for failing to implement engineering controls to reduce workplace noise exposure. According to the case facts, the company failed to carry out noise control measures recommended by a Noise Risk Assessor, including the installation of physical noise barriers in areas involving machine operations. The failure led to two workers suffering Noise Induced Hearing Loss (NIHL), which occupational health officers confirmed was caused by prolonged exposure to industrial noise.

items.title: Zahid: Rural and Regional Development Ministry saves RM250m, funds to go to MOF to support fuel subsidies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — The Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) has saved at least RM250 million through expenditure adjustments, including programme restructuring. In his speech, Ahmad Zahid also urged KKDW staff to adopt the National Rural Economy as a new mindset focused on raising incomes, expanding economic opportunities and improving the quality of life in rural communities. This is the foundation of the National Rural Economy, a comprehensive approach to transform rural areas from supporting zones into a new engine of national growth.

items.title: Malaysian Haj pilgrims urged to heed health advice amid rising heat in Saudi Arabia, says minister
items.summary: NILAI, May 4 — Malaysian Haj pilgrims have been advised to follow the advice of doctors, health officers and Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) personnel throughout their stay in the Holy Land to ensure their pilgrimage is carried out smoothly and safely. “Conditions in Saudi Arabia and the Holy Land are currently good. Earlier, he officiated the Islamic Media Arts Academy (ASMI) module book and course at Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia (USIM), which was also attended by USIM Pro-Chancellor Tunku Zain Al ‘Abidin Tuanku Muhriz.

items.title: Malaysia to pursue balanced economic diplomacy without compromising energy security, says Anwar
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that Malaysia adopts a balanced approach in economic diplomacy, prioritising national interests, including energy security. He said trade negotiations with strategic partners such as Australia are conducted on a reciprocal basis, taking into account Malaysia’s need to import raw materials such as phosphate for fertiliser production, while exporting urea. Anwar said Malaysia will continue to strengthen economic cooperation with key trading partners, including China and the United States, despite differences in views on geopolitical issues.

items.title: Home Ministry pushes to fast-track Pulau Malawali radar site to boost Sabah maritime security
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 4 — The Home Ministry (KDN) wants the construction of a Remote Sensor Site (RSS) on Pulau Malawali to be expedited, said its minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. Speaking at the KDN Monthly Assembly here today, he said the RSS is crucial in enhancing the country’s capability to detect threats in maritime areas, particularly along the east coast of Sabah. With radar capabilities, it will provide information on security and threats in the maritime area.

items.title: Amirudin: Royal institutions and ulama must be respected beyond politics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — Royal institutions and ulama should not be subjected to insults or abuse as they serve as symbols of unity and must be respected, Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. Amirudin said institutions such as the monarchy and the ulama should be respected beyond political differences. Earlier, Selangor police said they had received 280 reports over a viral Facebook post last month deemed insulting to the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah.
